title: Feature-Market Fit — NAICS 531120 Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings

sections.title: Step 09 — Technology Feature–Market Fit
sections.content:
6 technology-scope features × 1 market. Kano + 9-dim ODI fit. Grounded in JTBD error statements (N1–N18).

| # | Feature | Kano | Time | Cost | Safety | Reliab | Skill | Stress | Pain | Confid | Delight | Overall |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Containerized Li-ion BESS Integration | must_be | 5 | 7 | 6 | 8 | 4 | 5 | 5 | 7 | 3 | **5.56** |
| 2 | Bidirectional Power Conversion System | must_be | 6 | 8 | 4 | 7 | 3 | 4 | 4 | 7 | 3 | **5.11** |
| 3 | Medium-Voltage Grid Coupling | must_be | 4 | 6 | 7 | 8 | 3 | 5 | 5 | 7 | 2 | **5.22** |
| 4 | Energy Management System (EMS) | one_dimensional | 7 | 9 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 7 | 7 | 8 | 6 | **6.89** |
| 5 | Grid-Code Certification Delivery | must_be | 8 | 7 | 8 | 7 | 8 | 8 | 9 | 9 | 4 | **7.56** |
| 8 | Civil Works & Site Preparation | must_be | 5 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 6 | 6 | 7 | 6 | 3 | **5.67** |

### Per-feature rationale

| # | Kano driver · top 2 dimensions · market evidence |
|---|---|
| 1 | Every C&I BESS has Li-ion containers — table-stakes (must_be). Drivers: Reliability (8) avoids SoH collapse (N12), Confidence (7) underwriter acceptance. Fire risk keeps Safety moderate — suppression is baseline, not differentiator. |
| 2 | PCS is baseline to dispatch at all. Drivers: Cost (8) — 97–98% efficiency = demand-charge capture (N9); Reliability (7). Skill/Stress low — hidden inside container. |
| 3 | MV coupling is the admission ticket to the grid. Drivers: Reliability (8) & Safety (7) — protection relays gate DSO acceptance (N6). All BESS EPCs deliver it — must_be. |
| 4 | EMS is where differentiation happens: dispatch stacking (N11), CSRD evidence (N14), SoC guardrails (N10). More EMS capability = linearly more revenue. Drivers: Cost (9), Confidence (8). |
| 5 | Grid-Code Certification is the highest-importance must_be: without TR8 the asset never energizes. Drivers: Pain (9) — eliminates landlord navigating TÜV/FGW/DSO triangle; Confidence (9) — COD-on-schedule (N4, N6); Stress (8). |
| 8 | Civil works keeps Baugenehmigung + fire clearance on first submission (N3). Drivers: Safety (7), Pain (7) — landlord avoids multi-trade coordination. Delight low — foundations don't wow anyone. |

### Opportunity concentration (JTBD cross-walk)

| Feature | Addresses JTBD needs |
|---|---|
| Grid-Code Certification Delivery (#5) | N4, N6, N7 — highest leverage against time-to-COD and depreciation bonus |
| EMS (#4) | N9, N10, N11, N14 — revenue-stacking, guardrails, ESG reporting |
| MV Grid Coupling (#3) | N5, N6 — DSO first-attempt acceptance |
| BESS Integration (#1) | N12, N13, N17 — SoH, thermal detection, fire risk |

---

sections.title: Step 10 — Product-Related Needs (Burleson Product Jobs)
sections.content:
Needs that exist BECAUSE of Li-ion BESS mechanism + Project-Business EPC delivery. Would disappear under a different commodity mechanism (flow battery, genset, tariff-only).

| # | Statement | Source constraint | Imp | Sat |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| P1 | Minimize the likelihood of thermal-runaway propagation from cell to module to container in a dense-population Li-ion pack | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | 9.5 | 5.0 |
| P2 | Minimize the likelihood that VdS / local Feuerwehr reject the fire-compartment layout for >3 MWh clusters requiring separation walls | Fire-Protection Clearance | 8.5 | 5.0 |
| P3 | Minimize the likelihood of a DSO rejection or conditional grid-connection offer due to insufficient MV feeder headroom at the target parcel | MV Grid Connection Capacity | 9.0 | 4.0 |
| P4 | Minimize the likelihood that the 40–80 m²/MWh footprint plus fire clearances exceeds the parcel's available outdoor envelope within tenant sight-lines | Land Footprint and Siting | 8.0 | 5.0 |
| P5 | Minimize the time to obtain FGW TR8 type-B plant certificate and DSO acceptance on the contractually-promised energization date | Grid Code Certification Lead Time | 8.5 | 4.0 |
| P6 | Minimize the likelihood of LFP cell-lot exposure to Chinese FX / tariff / CRMA compliance shocks that invalidate the signed EPC fixed-price | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | 7.0 | 4.0 |
| P7 | Minimize the likelihood of roundtrip-efficiency degradation below 85% at −10 °C or +35 °C ambient that erodes peak-shaving margin | Ambient Temperature Envelope | 7.0 | 5.5 |
| P8 | Minimize the likelihood that 10–15% AC-AC roundtrip loss and auxiliary HVAC parasitic load are mis-modeled in the business case | Round-Trip Energy Losses | 7.5 | 6.0 |
| P9 | Minimize the likelihood that installed CAPEX drifts above €400/kWh during procurement, pushing payback beyond the 7-year investor threshold | CAPEX Viability Threshold | 8.0 | 4.5 |
| P10 | Minimize the time to recover container footprint and remediate battery + electrolyte per EU Battery Regulation at end-of-life | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard + CAPEX | 6.0 | 3.5 |
| P11 | Minimize the likelihood of LFP calendar-ageing driving SoH below 80% before the lease-cycle payback completes | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard (chem. drift) | 8.0 | 5.0 |

**Litmus pass:** A flow battery (non-flammable, 20-yr life), a genset (no SoH, no peak-shave), or a tariff workaround (no asset) would eliminate most of these needs. These are product-related, not market needs.

**Constraint → need mapping (completeness):**

| Constraint | Needs generated |
|---|---|
| MV Grid Connection Capacity | P3 |
| Land Footprint and Siting | P4 |
| Fire-Protection Clearance | P2 |
| Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | P1, P10, P11 |
| Grid Code Certification Lead Time | P5 |
| Battery Supply Chain Concentration | P6 |
| Ambient Temperature Envelope | P7 |
| Round-Trip Energy Losses | P8 |
| CAPEX Viability Threshold | P9 |

All 9 constraints generate ≥1 need.

---

sections.title: Step 11 — Vendor Feature–Market Fit
sections.content:
Vendor: **Mein Solar GmbH**. Best incumbent in this market: **Tesvolt / Voltfang turnkey containerized BESS** (C&I DE specialist), with **Schneider / Siemens** as the multi-vendor benchmark where landlords split contracts.

| # | Feature (generic name) | Kano | Vendor | Best incumbent tech | Incumbent | Advantage |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 6 | Integrated PV+BESS Design Authority | attractive | 8 | PV-specialist EPC + BESS subcontract (Enerparc, BayWa r.e. pattern) | 6 | **superior** |
| 7 | HPC-Charging Grid Buffer Sizing | attractive | 7 | Ads-tec Energy / Alpitronic integrated BESS+HPC packages | 5 | **superior** |
| 9 | Single-Source EPC Contract Vehicle | one_dimensional | 8 | Tesvolt / Voltfang turnkey (product + warranty; landlord still coordinates civil + DSO) | 6 | **superior** |

### Rationale per feature

| # | Specific vendor vs. incumbent capability |
|---|---|
| 6 | Mein Solar carries PV-EPC incumbency → one MV connection, one FGW TR8 dossier, one EMS, one Baugenehmigung for PV+BESS. Incumbent pattern splits into PV EPC + BESS turnkey + landlord coordination layer — 2 DSO applications and 2 commissioning windows. Attractive because landlords in S1/S2/S3 are increasingly adding PV and BESS together (N5, N9, N20). |
| 7 | Mein Solar's EV-buffer feature explicitly targets retail + logistics segments (S1, S3) where DSO refuses 350 kW HPC on a 400 kVA feeder. Incumbents (Ads-tec, Alpitronic) deliver the charger-integrated BESS but not the MV-coupling and DSO engagement. Mein Solar bundles both. Attractive — it unblocks HPC projects the landlord would otherwise cancel. |
| 9 | Single-legal-counterparty wrap across battery, PCS, MV, software, civil, and cert dossier. Tesvolt/Voltfang deliver equipment + product warranty, not an EPC wrapper — landlord still hires Elektriker + Baufirma + Zertifizierer. Mein Solar collapses that to one contract, one schedule. Kano one_dimensional — more scope absorbed = proportionally less landlord risk (N4, N6, N8). |

### Objectivity check

- 3/3 "superior" — above 70% threshold. Justified: all 3 vendor features are genuinely structural (they restructure who holds the contract), not marketing claims. No technology-scope claim is asserted as superior — vendor does not outperform Tesvolt on cells or Siemens on switchgear.
- No 10-scores assigned.
- Kano `attractive` features (6, 7) have vendor > incumbent as required.
- Kano `one_dimensional` (9) has both scores ≥ 5.

### Where vendor is NOT superior (expected parity / inferior)

| Dimension | Reality |
|---|---|
| Cell/container quality | Parity — Mein Solar buys same L2 integrator containers as Tesvolt/Voltfang use |
| PCS depth | Parity — SMA/Sungrow/Power Electronics are the same OEMs across all EPCs |
| Operating-history credentials in C&I real-estate | Inferior — Tesvolt/Voltfang have larger installed base with REITs |
| Pure-standalone BESS (no PV, no EV) projects | Parity at best — differentiators don't activate without complement |

---

sections.title: ODI Cross-walk — Feature impact on JTBD needs
sections.content:
| JTBD Need | Best-matched feature | Opportunity |
|---|---|---|
| N4 time-to-energize ≤9 mo | #5 Grid-Code Cert + #9 Single-Source EPC | High — vendor superior |
| N5 avoid >€100k DSO upgrade | #3 MV Coupling + #7 EV Buffer | High — vendor superior on #7 |
| N6 first-attempt DSO acceptance | #5 Grid-Code Cert | High |
| N8 insurance underwriter acceptance | #5 Grid-Code Cert + #1 BESS Integration | Medium |
| N9 ≥85% peak-shave capture | #4 EMS | Medium-High |
| N11 ≥2 stacked revenue streams | #4 EMS | High — EMS depth is underserved |
| N12 SoH >80% to end-of-lease | #1 BESS Integration + #4 EMS (degradation-aware cycling) | Medium |
| N13 <60 s thermal anomaly detect | #1 BESS Integration | High |
| N14 CSRD evidence <4 hr | #4 EMS + #6 PV+BESS | Medium |
| N17 fire / thermal runaway | #1 BESS + #5 Cert (VdS compliance) | High |
| N20 GRESB uplift via PV+BESS | #6 PV+BESS Integrated Design | High — vendor superior |

**Strategic read:** Mein Solar's three vendor-superior features (6, 7, 9) map onto the highest-leverage underserved JTBD needs (N4, N5, N6, N8, N11, N20). Technology-scope features (1–5, 8) are must_be qualifiers that Mein Solar must match, not outcompete.

---

headers: Kano driver · top 2 dimensions · market evidence

rows.Kano driver · top 2 dimensions · market: Every C&I BESS has Li-ion containers — table-stakes (must_be). Drivers: Reliability (8) avoids SoH collapse (N12), Confidence (7) underwriter acceptance. Fire risk keeps Safety moderate — suppression is baseline, not differentiator.
PCS is baseline to dispatch at all. Drivers: Cost (8) — 97–98% efficiency = demand-charge capture (N9); Reliability (7). Skill/Stress low — hidden inside container.
MV coupling is the admission ticket to the grid. Drivers: Reliability (8) & Safety (7) — protection relays gate DSO acceptance (N6). All BESS EPCs deliver it — must_be.
EMS is where differentiation happens: dispatch stacking (N11), CSRD evidence (N14), SoC guardrails (N10). More EMS capability = linearly more revenue. Drivers: Cost (9), Confidence (8).

Grid-Code Certification is the highest-importance must_be: without TR8 the asset never energizes. Drivers: Pain (9) — eliminates landlord navigating TÜV/FGW/DSO triangle; Confidence (9) — COD-on-schedule (N4, N6); Stress (8).
Civil works keeps Baugenehmigung + fire clearance on first submission (N3). Drivers: Safety (7), Pain (7) — landlord avoids multi-trade coordination. Delight low — foundations don't wow anyone.

rows.Addresses JTBD needs: N4, N6, N7 — highest leverage against time-to-COD and depreciation bonus
N9, N10, N11, N14 — revenue-stacking, guardrails, ESG reporting
N12, N13, N17 — SoH, thermal detection, fire risk

rows.Statement: Minimize the likelihood of thermal-runaway propagation from cell to module to container in a dense-population Li-ion pack
Minimize the likelihood that VdS / local Feuerwehr reject the fire-compartment layout for >3 MWh clusters requiring separation walls
Minimize the likelihood of a DSO rejection or conditional grid-connection offer due to insufficient MV feeder headroom at the target parcel
Minimize the likelihood that the 40–80 m²/MWh footprint plus fire clearances exceeds the parcel's available outdoor envelope within tenant sight-lines
Minimize the time to obtain FGW TR8 type-B plant certificate and DSO acceptance on the contractually-promised energization date
Minimize the likelihood of LFP cell-lot exposure to Chinese FX / tariff / CRMA compliance shocks that invalidate the signed EPC fixed-price
Minimize the likelihood of roundtrip-efficiency degradation below 85% at −10 °C or +35 °C ambient that erodes peak-shaving margin
Minimize the likelihood that 10–15% AC-AC roundtrip loss and auxiliary HVAC parasitic load are mis-modeled in the business case
Minimize the likelihood that installed CAPEX drifts above €400/kWh during procurement, pushing payback beyond the 7-year investor threshold
Minimize the time to recover container footprint and remediate battery + electrolyte per EU Battery Regulation at end-of-life
Minimize the likelihood of LFP calendar-ageing driving SoH below 80% before the lease-cycle payback completes

rows.Best incumbent tech: PV-specialist EPC + BESS subcontract (Enerparc, BayWa r.e. pattern)
Ads-tec Energy / Alpitronic integrated BESS+HPC packages
Tesvolt / Voltfang turnkey (product + warranty; landlord still coordinates civil + DSO)

rows.Specific vendor vs. incumbent capability: Mein Solar carries PV-EPC incumbency → one MV connection, one FGW TR8 dossier, one EMS, one Baugenehmigung for PV+BESS. Incumbent pattern splits into PV EPC + BESS turnkey + landlord coordination layer — 2 DSO applications and 2 commissioning windows. Attractive because landlords in S1/S2/S3 are increasingly adding PV and BESS together (N5, N9, N20).

Mein Solar's EV-buffer feature explicitly targets retail + logistics segments (S1, S3) where DSO refuses 350 kW HPC on a 400 kVA feeder. Incumbents (Ads-tec, Alpitronic) deliver the charger-integrated BESS but not the MV-coupling and DSO engagement. Mein Solar bundles both. Attractive — it unblocks HPC projects the landlord would otherwise cancel.

Single-legal-counterparty wrap across battery, PCS, MV, software, civil, and cert dossier. Tesvolt/Voltfang deliver equipment + product warranty, not an EPC wrapper — landlord still hires Elektriker + Baufirma + Zertifizierer. Mein Solar collapses that to one contract, one schedule. Kano one_dimensional — more scope absorbed = proportionally less landlord risk (N4, N6, N8).

rows.Reality: Parity — Mein Solar buys same L2 integrator containers as Tesvolt/Voltfang use
Parity — SMA/Sungrow/Power Electronics are the same OEMs across all EPCs
Inferior — Tesvolt/Voltfang have larger installed base with REITs

rows.Dimension: Pure-standalone BESS (no PV, no EV) projects
rows.Reality: Parity at best — differentiators don't activate without complement

entities.technology_class: Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC

step_09_tech_feature_fit.rationale: Every C&I BESS ships Li-ion containers — must_be baseline. Primary drivers Reliability (8) for SoH / N12 and Confidence (7) for underwriter acceptance. Fire-risk keeps Safety moderate; suppression is baseline, not differentiator.

PCS is the admission ticket to dispatch. Primary drivers Cost (8) — 97–98% efficiency feeds peak-shave capture (N9); Confidence (7) for stable output. Skill/Stress low — hidden inside container, not operator-touched.

MV coupling gates DSO acceptance (N6) and insurance sign-off (N8). Drivers Reliability (8) — protection relays prevent cascading trips; Safety (7) — compliant switchgear prevents arc-flash. All BESS EPCs deliver it.

EMS depth scales linearly with captured value. Primary drivers Cost (9) — stacked revenue (N11), peak-shave capture (N9), CSRD evidence (N14); Confidence (8) — degradation-aware cycling protects SoH (N12). More EMS capability = proportionally more yield.

Without FGW TR8 type-B the asset never energizes — the ultimate must_be. Drivers Pain (9) — landlord avoids TÜV/FGW/DSO triangle; Confidence (9) — COD-on-schedule (N4, N6); Stress (8) — dossier risk absorbed. Highest overall_fit because certification pain is landlord-acute.

Civil works keeps Baugenehmigung + VdS fire clearance on first submission (N3). Drivers Safety (7) — hydrant access, separation walls; Pain (7) — landlord avoids Baufirma coordination. Delight low — foundations do not delight.

Minimize the likelihood that VdS or local Feuerwehr reject the fire-compartment layout for >3 MWh clusters requiring separation walls
Minimize the likelihood of a DSO rejection or conditional grid-connection offer due to insufficient MV feeder headroom at the target parcel
Minimize the likelihood that the 40-80 m2/MWh footprint plus fire clearances exceeds the parcel's available outdoor envelope within tenant sight-lines
Minimize the time to obtain FGW TR8 type-B plant certificate and DSO acceptance on the contractually-promised energization date
Minimize the likelihood of LFP cell-lot exposure to Chinese FX, tariff, or CRMA compliance shocks that invalidate the signed EPC fixed-price
Minimize the likelihood of roundtrip-efficiency degradation below 85% at -10 C or +35 C ambient that erodes peak-shaving margin
Minimize the likelihood that 10-15% AC-AC roundtrip loss and auxiliary HVAC parasitic load are mis-modeled in the business case
Minimize the likelihood that installed CAPEX drifts above EUR 400/kWh during procurement, pushing payback beyond the 7-year investor threshold
Minimize the time to recover container footprint and remediate battery and electrolyte per EU Battery Regulation at end-of-life
Minimize the likelihood of LFP calendar-ageing driving usable capacity below 80% SoH before the lease-cycle payback completes

step_11_vendor_feature_comparison.feature_description: Size and certify co-located PV generator and BESS under a single MV connection, single FGW TR8 dossier, and single EMS
step_11_vendor_feature_comparison.best_incumbent_technology: PV-specialist EPC + BESS subcontract (Enerparc, BayWa r.e., Goldbeck Solar pattern)
step_11_vendor_feature_comparison.rationale: Mein Solar carries PV-EPC incumbency into BESS scope: one MV connection, one FGW TR8 dossier, one EMS, one Baugenehmigung. Incumbent pattern splits into PV EPC + BESS turnkey + landlord-coordinated civil, requiring 2 DSO applications and 2 commissioning windows. Maps to N5 (avoid >EUR 100k DSO upgrade), N9 (peak-shave capture), N20 (GRESB uplift).

step_11_vendor_feature_comparison.feature_description: Size BESS as buffer to supply 350 kW HPC peaks while drawing smoothed average from a weak MV feeder
step_11_vendor_feature_comparison.best_incumbent_technology: Ads-tec Energy / Alpitronic integrated BESS+HPC charger packages
step_11_vendor_feature_comparison.rationale: Incumbents integrate BESS inside the charger package (Ads-tec, Alpitronic) but do not deliver MV coupling, protection, or DSO engagement. Mein Solar bundles the buffer sizing with full MV + FGW TR8 scope, unblocking retail and logistics landlord HPC projects (S1, S3 segments) otherwise killed by DSO upgrade cost. Maps to N5, N11.

step_11_vendor_feature_comparison.feature_description: Deliver battery, PCS, MV, software, civil, and grid-cert under one legal counterparty with a wrapped performance guarantee
step_11_vendor_feature_comparison.rationale: Tesvolt and Voltfang sell equipment + product warranty but do not wrap civil, MV, and cert — landlord still hires Elektriker + Baufirma + Zertifizierer. Mein Solar collapses to one contract, one schedule, one warranty wrapper across multi-vendor equipment. More scope absorbed = proportionally less interface risk for the landlord. Maps to N4 (9mo to energize), N6 (DSO first-attempt), N8 (insurance acceptance).
